dependent_vars.definition: judged violations of the necessary-conditions reading (C [= Π(C))
dependent_vars.name: n_necessity_violations

dependent_vars.definition: judged violations of the sufficiency (equivalence) reading
dependent_vars.name: n_sufficiency_violations

n_planned.power: ≥0.90 to distinguish a true 10% from a true 20% violation rate at α=0.05 (P8 §1.6: h=0.28, n=200 ⇒ power ≈0.98)

endpoints.test: P1 HS3 two-decision Wilson rule (P8 §1.5 row 5, no TOST): the pin SURVIVES iff the necessity-violation-rate Wilson 95% upper bound ≤ 0.10; the KILL requires the lower bound > 0.10; anything between is INCONCLUSIVE and buys more annotations, not a verdict
same two-decision Wilson rule on the sufficiency-violation rate (the equivalence reading); Holm-corrected within F-secondary(g3)
Cohen's κ reported; κ < 0.4 trips INSTRUMENT-INVALID, never FAIL

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: P1 §4b row HS2–HS8 (verbatim): Measured range: R0 — no host model. Reasonable extrapolation envelope: Model-scale-free (properties of the kernel formalism); the relevant axis is KERNEL size: verdicts on 54–10³ records re-checked at 10⁴–10⁵ during bulk authoring (G6/G7 re-run as regression). Literature anchor + licensing assumption: Formal-fragment properties don't vary with observer scale; only corpus composition shifts them.
kill_criterion_verbatim: P1 §4 HS3 kill criterion (verbatim): 'Necessity failures >10% ⇒ defeasible-script stands, Π is lint, and HS2 auto-resolves to sidecar-only. Sufficiency failures >10% ⇒ equivalence dead, necessary-conditions only. Test: each rate against its 10% threshold by exact binomial with Wilson 95% bounds over the full judgment set (~200 instance judgments: powered ≥0.9 to distinguish a true 10% from a true 20% rate at α=0.05); a "survives" verdict requires the Wilson upper bound ≤10%, a kill requires the lower bound >10%, anything between is INCONCLUSIVE and buys more annotations, not a verdict. Inter-annotator κ reported.'

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1: digest = sha256 over the UTF-8 concatenation of one line per regular file under data/<corpus>/ (recursive; symlinks and directories excluded), each line '<sha256-of-file-bytes-hex>  <relpath>\n' with exactly two spaces, relpath POSIX-style ('/'-separated) relative to data/<corpus>/, lines sorted by the UTF-8 byte order of relpath; reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py
corpus_hashes.instance-descriptions: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:g3.materials (~20 concepts × ~10 instance descriptions; ops amendment before any final-phase run)

title: G3 — semantics-pin annotation study: necessity vs sufficiency violations over ~20 concepts × ~10 instances (HS3 / NF7)
